summary: The A90 Supra uses BMW's B58 engine, which is proving to be very reliable. Most concerns relate to the BMW-sourced electrical and interior components rather than the powertrain.

issues.name: B58 Coolant Loss
issues.description: Some A90 Supras experience gradual coolant loss without visible leaks. Often traced to the expansion tank or small hose connections at the rear of the engine.

issues.name: Differential Noise (Early 2020 Models)
issues.description: Some early 2020 models developed differential whine or clunking. Toyota issued a TSB and replaced differentials under warranty.

issues.name: Infotainment Quirks
issues.description: The BMW-sourced iDrive infotainment can have connectivity issues, occasional freezes, and incompatibility with some phones' Bluetooth profiles.

issues.name: ZF8 Transmission Shudder
issues.description: The ZF 8-speed automatic can develop a slight shudder or hesitation at low speeds. Often resolved with a transmission software update or fluid change.

issues.name: Fuel Pump Recall
issues.description: Toyota issued a recall for certain fuel pump assemblies that could fail and cause stalling. Verify recall has been completed.

buyingTips: The B58 engine is proving extremely reliable — it shares DNA with the BMW 340i/M340i and is well-understood
Verify all recalls have been completed — the fuel pump recall is the most critical
Check for aftermarket ECU tunes — the B58 responds so well to tuning that many owners flash and reflash. Look for signs
The 2021+ models received a manual transmission option and numerous improvements — later model years are preferred
Maintenance follows the BMW schedule — use BMW-spec oil and follow recommended intervals despite the Toyota badge
